findings.fix: Factor the counit naturality transport into an explicit lemma, or prove it by rewriting with the public `lTensor_counit_coact`/naturality API rather than unfolding tensor-map internals.
findings.issue: `prodCoact_counit_map` closes the counit transport with a brittle `simpa only` over implementation lemmas such as `LinearMap.lTensor_def`, `TensorProduct.map_map`, and `TensorProduct.map_tmul`.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "change \(f p\)\.1|change \(f p\)\.2" code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Product.lean` hits lines 584-585; `ComoduleCat.comp_apply` exists in `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/ComoduleCat.lean:191`.
findings.fix: Rewrite the hypotheses with the existing public lemmas `ComoduleCat.comp_apply`, `prodFst_apply`, and `prodSnd_apply`, or add explicit projection-composite lemmas and use those instead of `change`.
findings.issue: `prod_hom_ext` uses `change` to reinterpret projected categorical composites as pair components, relying on definitional equality through `ComoduleCat` wrappers.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "change f\.toLinearMap|change g\.toLinearMap" code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Product.lean` hits lines 600-602; the categorical application lemma is available at `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/ComoduleCat.lean:191`.
findings.fix: Use `ComoduleCat.comp_apply`, `prodInl_apply`, and `prodInr_apply` to obtain the pointwise equalities, then finish with linearity; avoid exposing the proof to the concrete representation of categorical morphisms.
findings.issue: `prod_hom_ext'` repeats the same wrapper-defeq pattern, using `change` to turn inclusion composites into `toLinearMap` evaluations.

summary: The proof quality issues are localized but material: one tensor counit proof still depends on low-level normalization, and the bundled extensionality proofs rely on `change` through categorical wrapper definitional equality despite available API lemmas.
